checks.finding: The requirements define deterministic behavior, exact inventory, and copy-runner failure semantics sufficiently for downstream verification.
No contradictory goals or acceptance outcomes were found across fail-closed, human-only installation, generated-module use, and non-transactional replacement.
Partial-install recovery is observable through fixed-index failure injection, exact prefix/suffix digests, exit 2, complete rollback, all prior digests, and post-install verification.
Build-time Python, approval limits, and Windows PowerShell 5.1 Full Language local-NTFS assumptions are explicit and unsupported hosts fail before replacement.
Agent staging, human protected-file publication, disposable manifest authority, and R-10 boundaries are explicit.
Every requirement has observable acceptance and no open decision blocks design or task decomposition.
